title: dianthus carthusianorum subsp. carsticus
intro: Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. carsticus, also known as Carthusian pink or red clover, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and saponins, which are believed to provide various health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, as well as helping to lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health. Dianthus carthusianorum subsp. carsticus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, making it a popular choice for those seeking natural remedies for their health concerns.
